why: A and B both correctly refuse since the K14479 context has nothing on SNAT idle-timeout, while C invents a plausible-but-ungrounded explanation; A is marginally crisper than B.

B accurately describes gtmd/big3d/iQuery-mesh grounded in the context and is the most concise; C is most complete but verbose with a slightly off citation and A invents a wrong K13690 version range (11.x-14.x).
All three fabricate specific per-platform key counts not present in the truncated K7467 context and cite wrong/non-FIPS platforms (i5820-DF etc.), but A invents fewer fabricated rows than B and C.

The context is empty HTML boilerplate, so B's honest admission that the docs lack details is the most faithful, while A and C give generic descriptions and append clouddocs links not actually in the provided context.

All correctly center on TMM-not-ready/unresolved-MAC per K000152426; B is the most tightly grounded in the context's actual cluster-state cause and least padded, while A and C add more generic troubleshooting.
B and C give accurate idle-timer-reset-on-traffic behavior from real knowledge; A invents a specific bogus GUI config path (System>Configuration>Device Configuration Groups), making it less accurate.

C is flatly wrong claiming iQuery is not involved in sync and confuses it with DNS Express query handling; A and B at least correctly tie iQuery to inter-device state exchange though both miss gtmd/big3d specifics, with A slightly cleaner.

All three honestly decline to fabricate a number and defer to vendor/F5 docs, which is correct refusal behavior; B is the most concise while A and C add slightly more filler (A's SafeNet/nCipher guess is mildly speculative).

All correctly tie ACCESS::ephemeral-auth to APM ephemeral authentication; B is accurate and appropriately scoped, while A and C drift into invented criteria (time-of-day/location) and SSO/cookie specifics that overstate certainty.

A gives generic-but-plausible causes and wrongly expands F5OS as OpenStack but otherwise stays sound, while B and C both misidentify F5OS as 'F5 Distributed Cloud Edge / TMOS for OS-10' which is incorrect; none surface the real UCS/TMM-not-ready cause.
